title: Women
questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'women'?
What are women best at?
What is the most annoying thing about women?
How would the world be different if all leaders were women?
Who are the greatest women in your country's history?
What do women like and dislike about being women?
Which country has the world's most beautiful women?
Do you think women should be allowed to fight in wars?
Joseph Conrad said: "Being a woman is a terribly difficult task, since it consists principally in dealing with men." What does this mean? Do you agree?
Harriet Beecher Stowe said: "Women are the real architects of society." Do you agree?
Are women equal to men?
Who's the greatest woman you know?
Why do women like fashion so much?
Are photos of women in advertising demeaning to women?
Are women from your culture luckier than women from other cultures?
What common characteristics do all women share?
Are women cleverer than men?
How will women be different 100 years from now?
Someone once said: "Women get the last word in every argument. Anything a man says after that is the beginning of a new argument." Do you agree?
Someone once said: "God created man before woman. But then you always make a rough draft before the final masterpiece." What does this mean? Do you agree?
